For more information, see the <3>role-b: For more information, see the <3>role-based access control documentation</3>.
{{label}} request and limit must be set: {{label}} request and limit must be set before {{label}} utilization can be set.
**Builder Images** are container images: **Builder Images** are container images that build source code for a particular language or framework.
**Devfiles** are sets of objects for cre: **Devfiles** are sets of objects for creating services, build configurations, and anything you have permission to create within a Project.
**Templates** are sets of objects for cr: **Templates** are sets of objects for creating services, build configurations, and anything you have permission to create within a Project.
<0>Tip:</0> Use the <2>kn func create</2: <0>Tip:</0> Use the <2>kn func create</2> command to create the serverless function.
A {{deploymentConfigLabel}} defines the: A {{deploymentConfigLabel}} defines the template for a Pod and manages deploying new Images or configuration changes.
A {{deploymentLabel}} enables declarativ: A {{deploymentLabel}} enables declarative updates for Pods and ReplicaSets.
A <1>BuildConfig</1> to build source fro: A <1>BuildConfig</1> to build source from a Git repository.
A <1>DeploymentConfig</1> to rollout new: A <1>DeploymentConfig</1> to rollout new revisions when the Image changes.
A <1>Service</1> to expose your workload: A <1>Service</1> to expose your workload inside the Cluster.
A Deployment enables declarative updates: A Deployment enables declarative updates for Pods and ReplicaSets.
A DeploymentConfig defines the template: A DeploymentConfig defines the template for a Pod and manages deploying new Images or configuration changes.
A GitHub App is already set up for this: A GitHub App is already set up for this cluster. To use it, install the GitHub app on your personal account or GitHub organization.
A list of actions that are not shown to: A list of actions that are not shown to users on the add page.
A list of software catalog types that ar: A list of software catalog types that are not shown to users.
A liveness probe checks if the Container: A liveness probe checks if the Container is still running. If the liveness probe fails the Container is killed.
A readiness probe checks if the Containe: A readiness probe checks if the Container is ready to handle requests. A failed readiness probe means that a Container should not receive any traffic from a proxy, even if it's running.
A startup probe checks if the applicatio: A startup probe checks if the application within the Container is started. If the startup probe fails the Container is killed.
A type of deployment that enables Server: A type of deployment that enables Serverless scaling to 0 when idle.
A unique name for the Project.
A unique name given to the component tha: A unique name given to the component that will be used to name associated resources.
Add frequently accessed resources to you: Add frequently accessed resources to your side navigation for quick access. Look for the
Add options failed to load. Check your c: Add options failed to load. Check your connection and reload the page.
Add shared applications, services, event: Add shared applications, services, event sources, or source-to-image builders to your Project from the software catalog. Cluster administrators can customize the content made available in the catalog.
Additional labels which are only added t: Additional labels which are only added to the Route resource.
Allows the builds to use a different pat: Allows the builds to use a different path to locate your Devfile, relative to the Context Dir field
Allows the builds to use a different path to locate your Dockerfile, relative to the Context Dir field.
An image that can carry out the deployme: An image that can carry out the deployment.
An optional <1>Route</1> to expose your: An optional <1>Route</1> to expose your workload outside the Cluster.
Another option to customize and standard: Another option to customize and standardize your development process. As an admin, you can disable the complete Software Catalog, or individual sub-catalogs (available as Types in the Software Catalog). Also here the "Search" and "Topology" will still show such resources.
Arguments to default image entry point: Arguments to default image entry point
As admin you can change the pinned resou: As admin you can change the pinned resources that are shown to users by default. Users can still override this configuration and add or reorder their pinned resources. As soon as a user changes the default settings, new default settings are no longer applied.
Auto deploy when deployment configuratio: Auto deploy when deployment configuration changes
Auto deploy when new Image is available: Auto deploy when new Image is available
Automatically build a new image when con: Automatically build a new image when config changes
Automatically build a new image when ima: Automatically build a new image when image changes
Automatically build a new Image when the: Automatically build a new Image when the Builder Image changes
Automatically configure a new Pipeline R: Automatically configure a new Pipeline Repository for your Git repository. This will automatically trigger new PipelineRuns on new commits or Pull Requests based on your configuration in your source code.
Autoscale window must be between 6s and: Autoscale window must be between 6s and 1h.
Browse for container images that support: Browse for container images that support a particular language or framework. Cluster administrators can customize the content made available in the catalog.
Browse for devfiles that support a parti: Browse for devfiles that support a particular language or framework. Cluster administrators can customize the content made available in the catalog.
Browse for templates that can deploy ser: Browse for templates that can deploy services, create builds, or create any resources the template enables. Cluster administrators can customize the content made available in the catalog.
Browse the catalog to discover and deplo: Browse the catalog to discover and deploy operator managed services
Browse the catalog to discover database: Browse the catalog to discover database services to add to your application
Browse the catalog to discover, deploy a: Browse the catalog to discover, deploy and connect to services
Build configuration describes build defi: Build configuration describes build definitions used for transforming source code into a runnable container image.
Build hooks allow you to run commands at: Build hooks allow you to run commands at the end of the build to verify the image.
Build option to use for transforming sou: Build option to use for transforming source code into a runnable container image.
Build using pipeline describes a process: Build using pipeline describes a process for transforming source code into a runnable container image. Pipelines support can be added using Red Hat OpenShift Pipelines Operator.
Build, deploy and configure a Pipeline R: Build, deploy and configure a Pipeline Repository
Builder image is not present on cluster: Builder image is not present on cluster
Builds triggered from this Build Configu: Builds triggered from this Build Configuration will run all at the same time. The order in which they will finish is not guaranteed.
Builds triggered from this Build Configuration will run one at the time, in the order they have been triggered.

Builds triggered from this Build Configuration will run one at the time. When a currently running build completes, the next build that will run is the latest build created. Other queued builds will be cancelled.
Changing to this builder image will upda: Changing to this builder image will update your associated Pipeline and remove any customization you may have applied.

Cluster Build Strategies define a shared: Cluster Build Strategies define a shared group of steps, needed to fullfil the application build process.

The lower limit for the number of Pods t: The lower limit for the number of Pods that can be set by autoscaler. If not specified defaults to 0.
The maximum amount of CPU the Container: The maximum amount of CPU the Container is allowed to use when running.
The maximum amount of Memory the Contain: The maximum amount of Memory the Container is allowed to use when running.
The maximum number of pods that can be s: The maximum number of pods that can be scheduled above the original number of pods while the rolling deployment is in progress. This can be either a percentage (10%) or a whole number (1).
The maximum number of pods that can be u: The maximum number of pods that can be unavailable during the rolling deployment. This can be either a percentage (10%) or a whole number (1).

Use the Core platform perspective to man: Use the Core platform perspective to manage workload storage, networking, cluster settings, and more. This may require additional user access.
Use the Developer perspective to build a: Use the Developer perspective to build applications and associated components and services, define how they work together, and monitor their health over time.
Use your Bitbucket App password. Use thi: Use your Bitbucket App password. Use this <2>link</2> to create a token with <4>Read and Write </4>scopes in <7>Account, Workspace membership, Projects, Issues, Pull requests and Webhooks</7>.
Use your Git Personal token. Create a to: Use your Git Personal token. Create a token with repo, public_repo & admin:repo_hook scopes and give your token an expiration, i.e 30d.

Use your Gitlab Personal access token. U: Use your Gitlab Personal access token. Use this <2>link</2> to create a token with <4>api</4> scope. Select the role as <6>Maintainer/Owner</6>. Give your token an expiration i.e 30d.
